title: ESM Packaging, Exports, Executable Entry, Artifact Inspection, and Platform Support · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A public science-learning team incrementally builds installable inspected Node Pokedex package from original fixed PokéAPI payloads while retaining prior TypeScript, HTTP, Git, repository-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of installable inspected Node Pokedex package while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Define package type, source and output roots, explicit extensions, imports, exports, internal boundaries, and supported Node resolution.
Publish an executable JavaScript entry with portable launcher behavior, thin process wiring, correct permissions, and no source-tree assumptions.
Use package files or ignore rules, inspect npm pack output, include license and docs, exclude secrets and residue, and verify dependency provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 3247 during an offline mobile-library catalog migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bind ESM source and emitted JavaScript to inspected packages, executable smoke, and platform behavior; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use budget must remain enforced; then reorder one Promise, waiter, cache, timeout, or queue settlement and exclude stale state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Pokedex Ts Upgrade Uninstall: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 2114 during a narrow-terminal community science event. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bind ESM source and emitted JavaScript to inspected packages, executable smoke, and platform behavior; each Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, and handle needs explicit ownership; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Document configuration and data locations, schema compatibility, backup, upgrade, rollback, uninstall, support, and retained user data.
steps.why: Retrieving Pokedex Ts Upgrade Uninstall through the concrete installable inspected Node Pokedex package case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document configuration and data locations, schema compatibility, backup, upgrade, rollback, uninstall, support, and retained user data.

hints: Name the Pokedex Ts Upgrade Uninstall precondition or invariant that the workshop case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A workshop TypeScript team handles case 2114 during a narrow-terminal community science event. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bind ESM source and emitted JavaScript to inspected packages, executable smoke, and platform behavior; each Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, and handle needs explicit ownership; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Document configuration and data locations, schema compatibility, backup, upgrade, rollback, uninstall, support, and retained user data.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: npm uninstall should remove every file the application created.

checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: pokedex-ts-upgrade-uninstall-jz5i48.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: pokedex-ts-upgrade-uninstall-jz5i48(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_pokedex_ts_upgrade_uninstall_jz5i48\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?targets < 2)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cleanInstalls !== targets)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?smokePassed !== targets)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?inspectedPacks !== targets)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
